meta.title: Skill Graph — Full Detail (Every Feature Expanded)
meta.description: All 42 external features across 7 frameworks, expanded with verbatim wording, metrics, and implementation notes. Zero curation.

props.title: Every Feature Expanded
props.subtitle: All 42 external features across 7 frameworks with verbatim wording, metrics, and notes. Your call on what to steal.

props.content: No decisions here. No 'superseded' labels. No 'Lev is stronger' summaries. Just every feature expanded with the exact wording from the parity YAMLs, the metrics the authors chose, and the implementation notes. Read, highlight the one sentence that's powerful, and cherry-pick.

### Lev Skills in Shape (11 total)

**`interview`** — Wizard-mode questioning skill for structured decision-making. Uses framework library (SCAMPER, First Principles, Systems Thinking). Guides through complex decisions one question at a time.

**`cdo`** — Adaptive multi-agent deliberation for cdo/think/deep/debug/parliament workflows. Fractal context loop (CONTEXT→PLAN→ACT→VERIFY) at every level. Mandatory Turn 0 context gathering (skills, codebase, web). Convergence requires evidence, not just agreement.

**`prior-art`** — Evidence-first provenance report from `cass`, repo docs, `.lev`, `~/.agents/diagrams`, `qmd`, `lev get`, or `rg`.

**`cass`** — Search wrapper around the external `cass` CLI for coding-agent session history. Uses `cass` in machine-readable mode (`--json` or `--robot`).

**`cass-memory`** — Procedural memory wrapper around the external `cm` CLI (CASS Memory System). Three-layer cognitive loop.

**`lev-research`** — When any research, search, or information gathering is needed.

**`sherlock`** — Sherlock Bytes: Deep Research Intelligence System.

**`codebase-archaeology`** — Systematically explore unfamiliar codebases to build working mental models. Use when onboarding to new project, "what does this do", or understanding legacy code.

**`skill-discovery`** — Local skills runtime lookup over the canonical Leviathan skills inventory. Uses `~/.agents/lev-skills.sh` + `~/.agents/skills-inventory.jsonl` as the source of truth.

**`search`** — Unified Search Router.

**`brainstorming`** — (deprecated, superseded by `interview`)

props.title: omx-01 · OMX · 4-lane operator model
props.content:
**Full name**: 4-lane operator model (deep-interview → ralplan → team/ralph → verify)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"The 4-lane model IS the day-to-day UX. Lev has the flows but not the unified surface."*

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/flows/

**Action**: adopt

props.title: omx-02 · OMX · Deep-interview with mathematical ambiguity scoring
props.content:
**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim — the juicy math)**: *"Weighted dimensions: intent 30%, outcome 25%, scope 20%, constraints 15%, success 10%. Depth profiles: quick ≤0.30, standard ≤0.20, deep ≤0.15."*

**What this gives you**: a single scalar ambiguity score. You stop interviewing when the score drops below the depth profile threshold. Quick mode cuts off at 0.30, deep mode requires 0.15.

**Lev equivalent**: N/A (pattern exists in worktree, not in core)

**Action**: adopt

props.title: ooo-01 · Ouroboros · Socratic interview with ambiguity scoring
props.content:
**Status**: partial

**Metrics (verbatim from ouroboros.yaml)**:
- Ambiguity gate threshold: **0.2**
- Convergence threshold: **0.95**
- Max generations: **30**
- Cost tiers: **3** (PAL Router)

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Lev has planning flows but no Socratic interview with mathematical ambiguity gate. DNA constraints are the equivalent but applied to code, not requirements."*

**The powerful one-liner**: *stop interviewing when ambiguity < 0.2, stop evolving when ontology similarity > 0.95.*

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/ (plan phase)

props.title: ooo-03 · Ouroboros · Double Diamond decomposition
props.content:
**Full name**: Double Diamond decomposition (Discover → Define → Design → Deliver)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"FlowMind flows implement this pattern. stabilization-loop.flow.yaml, plan-execute-verify. Not branded as Double Diamond."*

**The pattern**: two diamonds back-to-back. First diamond (Discover → Define) is divergent-then-convergent problem framing. Second diamond (Design → Deliver) is divergent-then-convergent solution building.

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/flows/

props.title: ooo-11 · Ouroboros · Brownfield auto-detection
props.content:
**Full name**: Brownfield auto-detection (scan existing repo patterns)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Fractal config resolver discovers project structure. No explicit brownfield scanner."*

**What it means**: when starting a session in an existing codebase, auto-detect the conventions, stack, and patterns already in use BEFORE asking the user questions. Reduces ambiguity from day 0.

**Lev equivalent**: core/config/ (fractal resolver)

props.title: omo-01 · OMO · Category routing
props.content:
**Full name**: Category routing (task classification → mode selection)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"OMO routes tasks to categories before execution. Lev has flow selection but not auto-classification."*

**Action**: extract

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/

### Lev Skills in Plan (11 total)

**`work`** — Use when starting any tracked work session, resuming from a handoff, planning, designing, building, researching, or closing a session. THE lifecycle orchestrator.

**`lev-plan`** — Plan entity lifecycle management for `.lev/pm/plans/`, including required frontmatter, state transitions, and operational commands.

**`arch`** — Think and reason like a software architect. Analyze systems through quality attributes, trade-off analysis, and architectural drivers. Use for system design, tech selection, ADRs, architecture reviews, C4 descriptions, fitness functions, and PR review.

**`lev-cdo`** — Lev-specific CDO deliberation. Injects architecture context into cdo agent briefs. Use for architecture decisions, protocol design, module placement, and cross-cutting concerns in ~/lev.

**`cdo-team`** — Team-driven CDO deliberation using Claude Code team/subagent primitives. Teammates = persistent CDO role streams. Subagents = ephemeral sandboxed workers. Plans before executing.

**`lev-align`** — Project alignment validator. Loads .lev/validation-gates.yaml and checks current work against all applicable gates. Reports pass/fail/aspirational per gate. Integrates with /work ALIGN phase.

**`review-queue`** — Batch entity review dashboard with semantic prior art analysis.

**`lev-roadmap`** — Refresh Leviathan operational roadmap with done/not-done triage, critical-path ordering, and rolling next-10 steps while keeping stable destinations in docs/VISION.md.

**`lev-sdlc`** — Thin skill documenting the `lev sdlc *` command surface, SDLC plugin profiles, and related work/review loop commands in Leviathan.

**`planning-workflow`** — Comprehensive markdown planning methodology for software projects. Use when starting a new project, creating implementation plans, or refining architecture before coding.

**`writing-plans`** — Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code.

**`validate`** — Anti-Hallucination Validation Framework.

props.title: omx-05 · OMX · RALPLAN consensus planning
props.content:
**Full name**: RALPLAN consensus planning (structured deliberation)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"CDO is more powerful. RALPLAN has cleaner operator UX."*

**Lev equivalent**: CDO deliberation skills

**Action**: merge

**What to steal**: the operator UX. CDO has depth but RALPLAN has ceremony — the way it presents options to the operator. The wording of the consensus prompts. The flow of "propose → debate → converge" as a visible ritual instead of an internal algorithm.

props.title: ooo-05 · Ouroboros · Evolutionary loop with ontology convergence
props.content:
**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"eval-harness produce→judge→compare→keep_or_discard is the same pattern. HOLD state = collect more samples. But no ontology similarity metric."*

**Convergence metric**: stop evolving when ontology similarity >= **0.95**. Computed as `name_overlap + type_match + exact_match`.

**The powerful one-liner**: *the loop terminates when the next generation's ontology is 95% the same as the previous — nothing new to discover.*

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/flows/eval-harness.flow.yaml

props.title: ooo-10 · Ouroboros · Drift measurement
props.content:
**Status**: partial

**Composite drift score formula (verbatim)**: `goal 50% + constraint 30% + ontology 20%`

**Brownfield variant**: `goal 40%, constraint 30%, success criteria 30%, context 15%`

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"ValidatorChain detects constraint drift. No composite drift score with weighted dimensions yet."*

**The powerful pattern**: instead of binary pass/fail gates, compute a weighted score across multiple dimensions. You can see not just 'is this broken' but 'is this drifting away from intent.'

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/validator/ + dna/gates.yaml

props.label: LANE · DELIVER
props.title: Deliver — Every Feature In Full
props.subtitle: 13 Lev skills + 11 external features + 7 roles (biggest lane)

props.content:
### Lev Skills in Deliver (13 total)

**`agent-browser`** — Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction.

**`ntm`** — Named Tmux Manager for orchestrating Claude, Codex, and Gemini agents in tmux sessions. Use when you need to spawn, attach, message, inspect, or doctor a multi-agent tmux workspace.

**`vibing-with-ntm`** — Run NTM swarms with Agent Mail, Beads, and BV. Use when sending marching orders, tending parallel coding sessions, coordinating work claims and reservations, or running review loops for an NTM swarm.

**`lev-workshop`** — Workshop lifecycle for building and evolving plugins, hooks, and integrations. Four phases: intake → analysis → POC → poly integration.

**`subagent-driven-development`** — Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session.

**`dispatching-parallel-agents`** — Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies.

**`multi-agent-swarm-workflow`** — Run multi-agent coding swarms using NTM, Agent Mail, Beads, and BV. Use when spawning agent swarms, coordinating parallel implementation, sending marching orders, or running quality review loops.

**`test-driven-development`** — Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code.

**`systematic-debugging`** — Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes.

**`multi-pass-bug-hunting`** — Systematic audit-fix-rescan cycle for comprehensive bug elimination. Use when code review, deep audit, "find all bugs", or pre-release hardening.

**`autodev-loop`** — Autonomous development loop. Prompt generator + cron scheduler that delegates execution to /work, flow definitions, and bd.

**`executing-plans`** — Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints.

**`simplify`** — Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.

props.title: omx-03 · OMX · Tmux team lifecycle
props.content:
**Full name**: Tmux team lifecycle (N workers, shared task list, commit ledger)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"NTM exists. OMX adds team state management, worker health, auto-nudge."*

**What to steal**: the commit ledger pattern. A shared append-only log of what each worker committed, visible to all teammates. Plus the auto-nudge — when a worker stalls (no output for N minutes), automatically ping it with "still alive?".

**Lev equivalent**: NTM (Named Tmux Manager)

**Action**: merge

props.title: omx-04 · OMX · Ralph persistent loop
props.content:
**Full name**: Ralph persistent loop with architect verification

**Status**: implemented

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Same pattern, same name. lev-ralph is the canonical implementation."*

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/flows/lev-ralph.flow.yaml

props.title: omx-06 · OMX · Machine API
props.content:
**Full name**: Machine API (programmatic mode control)

**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"OMX exposes mode switching via machine-readable API. Lev uses CLI only."*

**What it means**: you can programmatically say "switch to deep-interview mode" or "start ralph loop" from another process without shelling out. The API is structured — pass a mode name and params, get back a handle to the running session.

**Action**: build

props.title: omx-09 · OMX · Auto-nudge / autopilot
props.content:
**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Similar concept. OMX has tighter tmux integration."*

**What it means**: detect stalled workers and nudge them with continuation prompts. Autopilot decides when to keep going vs when to ask the human.

**Lev equivalent**: autodev-loop skill

**Action**: merge

props.title: omc-01 · OMC · Claude Code team shell
props.content:
**Full name**: Claude Code team shell (provider-flavor team lifecycle)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Claude Code native team patterns. Lev uses Claude Code as an adapter, not the team surface."*

**What to think about**: should Claude Code's native team primitives be the team surface, or is that wrong because it locks us to one provider?

**Lev equivalent**: NTM + Agent tool

props.title: omc-03 · OMC · Provider-specific optimizations
props.content:
**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"OMC optimizes specifically for Claude Code. Lev is adapter-agnostic."*

**Trade-off**: adapter-agnostic is portable but loses per-provider tuning. OMC shows what you get when you DO optimize for one.

props.title: ooo-07 · Ouroboros · PAL Router
props.content:
**Full name**: PAL Router (3-tier cost optimization 1x/10x/30x)

**Status**: implemented

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Lev has adapter + model selection per node. lev-ralph uses haiku for triage, sonnet for work, opus for review. Same pattern."*

**The powerful framing (what to steal)**: the **1x/10x/30x** cost labels. Instead of naming models, name cost tiers. Then: triage runs at 1x (cheap sampling), work at 10x, review at 30x.

**Lev equivalent**: core/harness/src/ (adapter selection)

props.title: ooo-08 · Ouroboros · Ralph persistent loop
props.content:
**Status**: implemented

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"lev-ralph IS this. Same name even. Work→gate→review→done. Fresh context per iteration."*

**The convergent naming**: two independent projects (OMX and Ouroboros) both built this pattern and both named it "ralph." When two people independently invent the same name, the pattern is real.

props.title: ooo-09 · Ouroboros · Event sourcing with checkpoint recovery
props.content:
**Status**: implemented

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"LevEvent bus with append-only JSONL. AgentFS tool call audit trail. C2 Non-Commutation."*

**The theoretical grounding**: C2 Non-Commutation means the order of operations matters — you can't freely rearrange events. Event sourcing enforces this by making the log the source of truth.

**Lev equivalent**: core/event-bus/ + LevEvent JSONL

props.title: omo-02 · OMO · Planner/executor split
props.content:
**Status**: implemented

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Lev already has this pattern in multiple flows."*

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/core-sdlc/flows/ (plan-execute-verify)

props.title: hm-03 · Hermes · ACP editor integration
props.content:
**Full name**: ACP editor integration (VS Code, Zed, JetBrains)

**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"STEAL/WRAP — first-class editor integration via registry JSON."*

**ACP**: Agent Communication Protocol. A standard way for editors to talk to agents, so you don't have to build a bespoke integration per editor.

**Action**: steal

props.label: LANE · CLOSE
props.title: Close — Every Feature In Full
props.subtitle: 11 Lev skills + 8 external features + 6 roles (most framework overlap)

props.content:
### Lev Skills in Close (11 total)

**`handoff`** — Session close ceremony. Writes workstream state + pbcopy resume prompt. Use when closing a session, before /clear, or when the stop hook fires.

**`lev-align`** — Project alignment validator. Loads .lev/validation-gates.yaml and checks current work against all applicable gates.

**`verification-before-completion`** — Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs — requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always.

**`requesting-code-review`** — Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements.

**`receiving-code-review`** — Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable — requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation.

**`codebase-audit`** — Domain-parameterized codebase auditing (security, UX, performance, API, copy, CLI).

**`ubs`** — Static-analysis quality-gate wrapper around the external `ubs` CLI (Ultimate Bug Scanner). Runs `ubs` on staged, diffed, or explicitly changed files.

**`de-slopify`** — Remove telltale signs of AI-generated "slop" writing from documentation.

**`finishing-a-development-branch`** — Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work — guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup.

**`changelog-md-workmanship`** — Rebuild CHANGELOG.md files and release histories from git, tags, releases, and issue trackers.

**`readme-writing`** — Craft professional README.md files for GitHub open source projects.

props.title: ooo-04 · Ouroboros · 3-stage eval gate
props.content:
**Full name**: 3-stage eval gate (Mechanical → Semantic → Multi-Model)

**Status**: implemented

**The 3 stages (verbatim)**:
- **Mechanical** — does it compile? do tests pass? exit code 0?
- **Semantic** — does the output match the intended meaning? (metric-based)
- **Multi-Model** — do multiple independent LLMs agree the output is good?

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"EvalStrategy has 4 kinds: gate-based (mechanical), metric-based (semantic), llm-as-judge, adversarial. Eval harness flow implements the gating."*

**The powerful sequencing**: Mechanical is cheap, fails fast, catches 80% of broken output. Semantic is medium cost. Multi-Model is expensive — only runs if Mechanical + Semantic both pass.

**What to steal**: the naming. "Mechanical/Semantic/Multi-Model" is clearer than "gate/metric/judge."

**Lev equivalent**: core/orchestration/src/eval/types.ts

props.title: ooo-06 · Ouroboros · Nine agent personas
props.content:
**Full name**: Nine agent personas (Socratic, Ontologist, Contrarian, etc.)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Lev has tribunal (multi-model), cdo (deliberation), adversarial review. Not branded as named personas. The adversarial flowmind IS the Contrarian."*

**The 9 named personas worth considering**: Socratic (questioner), Ontologist (taxonomist), Contrarian (adversarial), plus 6 more you could lift directly as role prompts.

**What to steal**: named personas give operator-facing labels. Instead of "tribunal with adversarial mode" you say "call the Contrarian." The names become vocabulary.

**Lev equivalent**: skills/ (tribunal, cdo, adversarial-review)

props.title: omc-02 · OMC · Worker health monitoring and HUD
props.content:
**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Real-time worker health in team execution. Missing in Lev."*

**Action**: steal

**Lev equivalent**: N/A

props.title: omx-07 · OMX · HUD status display
props.content:
**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Real-time execution status overlay. Lev has no comparable surface."*

**Action**: steal

**What it means**: a persistent status surface that always shows what mode you're in, what agents are running, what the budget looks like. Not a log — a dashboard.

props.title: omo-03 · OMO · Hash-anchored edits
props.content:
**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"Content-addressed edit references. Interesting for deterministic replay."*

**What it means**: instead of "edit line 42 of file.py", anchor the edit to a content hash of the surrounding context. If the file changes, the anchor finds the new location by content, not line number. Deterministic replay even after drift.

**Action**: evaluate

props.title: hm-02 · Hermes · Doctor/status
props.content:
**Full name**: Doctor/status (hermes doctor, hermes status)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"STEAL — profile-aware runtime health. Lev has gates but no consumer health shell."*

**The powerful framing**: `hermes doctor` is a consumer-facing command that says "here's what's wrong with your setup" in plain language. Unlike gates which say pass/fail, doctor says "your Node version is old, your API key is missing, here's the fix."

**Action**: steal

**Lev equivalent**: plugins/validator/

props.title: oc-01 · OpenClaw · Gateway as typed WS control plane
props.content:
**Full name**: Gateway as typed WS control plane + OpenAI-compatible HTTP

**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"STEAL — polished single control-plane shell. Lev has no comparable gateway product."*

**What it means**: one WebSocket gateway that every client connects to. Strongly typed protocol. Plus an OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint for drop-in compatibility with any tool that speaks OpenAI's API.

**Action**: steal

props.title: oc-02 · OpenClaw · Onboarding wizard
props.content:
**Full name**: Onboarding wizard (openclaw onboard)

**Status**: missing

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"STEAL ceremony + sequencing. Consumer-grade setup flow. Lev has fragmented setup."*

**What to steal**: the ceremony. The sense that starting with the tool is a guided experience, not a README hunt.

**Action**: steal

props.title: oc-03 · OpenClaw · Doctor/repair/migration surface
props.content:
**Full name**: Doctor/repair/migration surface (openclaw doctor)

**Status**: partial

**Notes (verbatim)**: *"STEAL — Lev has validation gates but no consumer health shell. lev doctor exists but not polished."*

**Action**: steal

props.label: METRICS GLOSSARY
props.title: The Numbers Worth Stealing
props.subtitle: Verbatim from parity files — the specific thresholds and weights that make these patterns concrete

props.content:
**Ambiguity scoring (OMX omx-02):**

- Weighted dimensions: **intent 30% + outcome 25% + scope 20% + constraints 15% + success 10%**
- Depth profiles: **quick ≤0.30, standard ≤0.20, deep ≤0.15**

**Ambiguity scoring (Ouroboros ooo-01):**

- Ambiguity gate threshold: **0.2**
- Convergence threshold: **0.95**
- Max generations: **30**
- Cost tiers: **3** (PAL Router)

**Drift measurement (Ouroboros ooo-10):**

- Greenfield: `goal 50% + constraint 30% + ontology 20%`
- Brownfield: `goal 40%, constraint 30%, success criteria 30%, context 15%`

**PAL Router tiers (Ouroboros ooo-07):**

- **1x / 10x / 30x** cost labels — triage/work/review
- Mapped to haiku/sonnet/opus in lev-ralph

**3-stage eval gate (Ouroboros ooo-04):**

- **Mechanical** (compile, exit code) → **Semantic** (metric-based match) → **Multi-Model** (LLM consensus)
- Run in order, cheapest first. Multi-Model only if Mechanical + Semantic both pass.

**Convergence / evolutionary loop (Ouroboros ooo-05):**

- Stop when ontology similarity >= **0.95**
- Formula: `name_overlap + type_match + exact_match`
- HOLD state = collect more samples before deciding

### The one-sentence powerful takeaways

1. *"Stop interviewing when ambiguity < 0.2"* (Ouroboros)
2. *"Stop evolving when ontology similarity > 0.95"* (Ouroboros)
3. *"Weight dimensions: intent 30% + outcome 25% + scope 20% + constraints 15% + success 10%"* (OMX)
4. *"Cost tiers: 1x triage / 10x work / 30x review"* (Ouroboros)
5. *"Gate stages: Mechanical → Semantic → Multi-Model"* (Ouroboros)
6. *"Drift = goal 50% + constraint 30% + ontology 20%"* (Ouroboros)
7. *"Auto-nudge when worker stalls"* (OMX)
8. *"Commit ledger shared across team"* (OMX)
9. *"Doctor says what's wrong in plain language"* (Hermes)
10. *"Onboarding is ceremony, not README"* (OpenClaw)
